element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Avoiding or eliminating threats via shooting/melee
Floor navigation, timing with moving platforms
Environmental damage to enemies or light control
Heightened difficulty, limited visibility for enemy detection
Hazard avoidance or tactical enemy elimination
Increased enemy aggression, slower elevator response
Final objective after all documents collected
Mandatory backtracking to highest uncollected floor if exit attempted early

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully collecting document from red door
Entering darkness section and surviving it
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy agent emergence from closed doors
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive tension escalation: initial safe descent interrupted by scattered enemy encounters, intensifying as alarm triggers mid-level forcing completion under time pressure with heightened enemy aggression and sluggish elevator response

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Red-marked doors indicate secret documents; non-red doors lead to secondary areas
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Light fixtures can be shot to disable overhead lighting, creating tactical darkness zones

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate introduction with basic enemies and simple elevator/stair traversal, safe descent opportunity before alarm pressure begins
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy density and aggression, introduction of darkness sections reducing visibility, time pressure via alarm system triggering
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained high aggression, mandatory document collection enforcement forcing revisits to earlier floors, combined hazards (darkness + enemies + elevator timing)
notable_spikes: Alarm trigger (enemy aggression increase, elevator slowdown)

notable_level_patterns.description: 30-story building navigation from roof to ground via elevators/stairs, forced vertical progression interrupted by horizontal floor exploration for document collection
All documents must be collected before exit is available; incomplete collection forces player back to highest uncollected floor, creating mandatory replay loop
Elevators serve triple purpose: transportation, moving platform for combat positioning, and crushing hazard for both player and enemies
Designated floors with lights out reduce sight range, forcing adapted enemy detection strategies without visual confirmation
Alarm system activates during extended play, increasing enemy aggression and reducing elevator responsiveness, forcing speed-focused decision-making
